title: jacobaea aquatica subsp. ornata
intro: Jacobaea aquatica subsp. ornata, also known as Jacob's Ladder or Ornate Marsh Senecio, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The health benefits of Jacobaea aquatica subsp. ornata include its potential to reduce inflammation, treat skin conditions, and act as an antimicrobial agent. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically proven.
